macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices spike on US-Iran military exchange while a US court strikes down Trump's tariffs, removing import cost pressure on US-listed importers.
primary_drivers: Middle East supply risk premium pushing crude higher
Removal of tariff cost passthrough on importers/retailers

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major; upstream earnings rise ~linearly with Brent. Direct beneficiary of Iran-driven supply risk premium.
High-beta US E&P with Permian leverage; among the most crude-sensitive names per dollar of oil move, no hedging drag vs peers.
Direct prime on missile defense (THAAD, PAC-3) and munitions resupply; Iran kinetic exchanges historically drive immediate order-flow expectations.
Patriot/Stinger/Tomahawk supplier; Middle East escalation directly increases munitions replenishment pipeline.
Five Below sources ~60% from China; tariff strikedown materially reduces COGS pressure that had been compressing gross margin guidance.
Consumer electronics retailer with heavy China-sourced SKUs (TVs, appliances); tariff removal lifts gross margin outlook directly.
Significant Vietnam/China footwear and apparel imports; tariff rejection removes a known earnings headwind embedded in sell-side models.
Oilfield services leverage to higher crude → US E&P capex; sustained $80+ Brent reactivates frac/completion demand.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; spike in crude on Iran tensions directly compresses near-term margins, and Delta hedges less than peers.
Southwest's hedge book has rolled off in recent years; jet fuel exposure makes it acutely sensitive to a sustained crude move.
Cruise operator burning bunker fuel; oil spike hits opex while Middle East tensions also threaten itinerary disruption.
Domestic steel producer whose pricing power relies on tariff-protected import parity; ruling pressures steel price deck.
Cleveland-Cliffs is the most tariff-levered US flat-rolled steelmaker; management has explicitly cited tariffs as supportive of domestic pricing.
First Solar's premium valuation rests partially on tariff/AD-CVD walls against Asian solar imports; broad tariff strikedown raises overhang risk on pricing.

headlines: Oil futures rise after U.S., Iran trade fire; stock futures quiet after court rejects Trump’s tariffs
